insight: For Spain, the hydration breaks in the 2026 World Cup have had a neutral impact on their momentum, with an average change of zero. This means that after a hydration break, Spain's performance has neither significantly improved nor declined, indicating that they are able to maintain their level of play. When Spain was pressing before a hydration break, which happened twice, they were able to gain momentum in one of those instances, suggesting that they can use the break to regroup and potentially launch a stronger attack afterwards. Overall, the hydration breaks have not provided a noticeable advantage or disadvantage for Spain, allowing them to largely dictate their own pace and strategy throughout the match.
